title: ARC and ownership semantics
description: Automatic Reference Counting frees an object when its strong reference count hits zero; cycles of strong references never reach zero and leak.
body: Use when reasoning about memory. ARC deallocates an object when no strong references remain; there is no tracing garbage collector, so two objects that strongly reference each other (or a closure capturing its owner) form a cycle that is never freed. weak references become nil when the target deallocates; unowned assume the target outlives the reference. Do not assume leaks get collected later. Actions: use weak/unowned to break ownership cycles. Validation signals include deallocation occurring at the expected time. Risk boundary: because nothing reclaims cycles, a single strong cycle in a frequently-created view controller leaks unboundedly until the app is killed — ownership must be designed, not left to chance.
